summary.intent: Add --trust flag to the Cursor Agent CLI invocation so evaluations work on remote/headless servers that block due to workspace trust prompts. Also ensure the frontend remediation command display reflects the actual invocation.
summary.outcome: Added --trust to the base args array in cursor-provider.ts and updated the hardcoded cursor writeCommand in RemediateTab.tsx to match. Both changes committed together with 1329 tests passing and lint clean.

repo: Cursor CLI requires --trust for non-interactive/headless use; analogous to Claude's --dangerously-skip-permissions and Codex's --full-auto --skip-git-repo-check
The frontend RemediateTab.tsx hardcodes command strings for display purposes and must be kept manually in sync with backend provider args
There is also an embedded frontend under src/web/ that should be checked when updating command display strings
Test suite runs 1329 tests via `bun run test`; lint via `bun run lint`

code.finding: Base args array for Cursor CLI; --trust added here applies to all invocations regardless of writeMode
Hardcoded cursor writeCommand for frontend display; must mirror the backend provider args (now: agent -p --output-format json --trust --force)

workflow: When changing a CLI provider's base args, always grep the frontend for hardcoded command display strings that duplicate those args
rtk prefix is used to run commands in the remote/target environment

friction: Frontend command display string was a non-obvious second location that required multiple grep passes to find — it diverged silently from the backend
open_items: Verbose log near line 95 (description of base command before write mode flags) was left as-is; worth revisiting if it causes confusion in logs
src/web/ embedded frontend was checked and clear, but should be monitored if it gains its own command display components
